key_insight: A coupled LSTM + physics-informed GNN can reconstruct nonlinear, history-dependent local stress fields by separating temporal constitutive memory from spatial equilibrium-consistent field prediction.
engineering_relevance: This enables fast, mesh-agnostic surrogate stress recovery for multiscale simulations of heterogeneous materials, reducing finite-element cost while preserving path-dependent mechanics.
method: LSTM encodes macroscopic stress-strain loading history into a compact hidden state.
Physics-informed GNN/MeshGraphNet reconstructs spatial stress fields on the microstructure mesh.
Training uses a warm-up weighted loss that balances reconstruction accuracy with a discrete divergence-based equilibrium penalty.
evidence_signal: Trained on 10,000 non-proportional loading paths for a plate-with-a-hole elasto-plastic microstructure, the model reports about a 1000× speedup over FEM and generalizes to sequences twice as long with 1.9% cumulative error.
watch_item: The approach depends on training data quality and a carefully tuned loss-weight schedule; fixed-weight or out-of-distribution loading conditions may reduce convergence or accuracy.
paper_title: Non-linear mechanical field reconstruction coupling recurrent neural networks with physics-informed graph neural networks
